title: Cisco ISE PSN Authentication-Per-Second (TPS) SLO and Capacity Headroom

compliance.controlObjective: PSN capacity headroom evidence for service-level objective attainment.

compliance.assurance_rationale: DORA Art.7 ICT risk — capacity planning.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Generate 6,000 authentications per minute against an SNS-3715 (5,000 TPS rated). UC-17.1.77 fires.
controlTest.negativeScenario: PSN running at 50% capacity. No fire.

dataSources: ISE syslog `index=nac sourcetype=cisco:ise:syslog` *Passed-Authentications* (5200) and *Failed-Authentications* (5400) — high-volume base searches. Use `_time` bucketing to compute per-PSN TPS. Cross-reference Cisco-published PSN sizing data: SNS-3715 = 5,000 TPS, SNS-3725 = 10,000 TPS, SNS-3755 = 20,000 TPS, SNS-3795 = 50,000 TPS.
spl:
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode IN (5200, 5400)
| bin _time span=1m
| stats count as auth_count by _time, host
| eval tps=round(auth_count/60, 1)
| lookup ise_psn_capacity.csv host OUTPUT psn_max_tps
| eval headroom_pct=round(((psn_max_tps - tps) / psn_max_tps) * 100, 1)
| where headroom_pct < 30
description: Tracks per-PSN authentication-per-second rate against the platform's published TPS ceiling. Alerts when sustained headroom falls below 30%.
value: PSNs that approach their TPS ceiling start dropping requests. Headroom monitoring lets capacity planning add nodes before users feel the pain — and prevents the worst-case scenario where ISE silently drops auth requests during a busy morning.
visualization: Per-PSN TPS gauge; headroom trend; capacity-utilization heatmap.

references.title: Cisco SNS-3700 Series Datasheet — TPS Specs

knownFalsePositives:
(a) Morning login storm peaks (8-9am) often dip below 30% briefly — tune.
(b) Maintenance on neighbor PSN concentrates load.
mitreAttack: N/A (SLO monitoring — measures ISE Policy Service Node latency, no adversary technique)
grandmaExplanation: Each ISE box can handle a maximum number of logins per second. We watch how close each box is to its limit — and warn before it gets crowded so we can add another box.
